redGreenRefactorEvidence: Red: unit tests for resolve_db_identity and integration tests for local wrapper written before implementation.
Green: safe_alembic_helpers.resolve_db_identity + safe-alembic-upgrade-local.sh pass 817 pytest tests.
Refactor: shared helpers reused; production safe-alembic-upgrade.sh unchanged.
implementationSummary: Added safe-alembic-upgrade-local.sh local migration gate with DB-identity resolver, pg_dump backup to ~/.juli-backups, interactive confirmation, and shared row-count verification via safe_alembic_helpers.py. Updated README and backend deploy runbook.
assumptions: Alembic migrations remain schema-only per CONTEXT.md; row-count gate detects accidental data loss, not in-place corruption.
Local developers have pg_dump available (same requirement as production safe-alembic-upgrade.sh).
risks: Local pg_dump adds latency to every local migration run.
Interactive confirmation may interrupt scripted local workflows unless --yes or SAFE_MIGRATE_YES=1 is set.
